Field.Label: Indigo Server IP Address:
Field.Description: Your Indigo server IP (e.g. 192.168.100.160). Leave blank for auto-detect. Enter this IP in your Ecowitt gateway settings.

Field.Label: HTTP Server Port:
Field.Description: Port the plugin listens on (1024-65535). Configure your gateway to POST to this port. Default: 8088.

Field.Label: Listen Address:
Field.Description: Use "All Interfaces" for normal operation.

Field.Label: Distance Units:
Field.Description: Used for lightning strike distance.

Field.Label: Decimal Places:
Field.Description: Decimal precision for temperature, wind, pressure, rain values.

Field.Label: Auto-Create Devices:
Field.Description: Automatically create Indigo devices when new sensors are first detected.

Field.Label: Device Folder:
Field.Description: Folder where new devices will be created. Select Root for no folder.

Field.Label: Device Name Prefix:
Field.Description: Prefix for auto-created device names (e.g. "Ecowitt Outdoor Sensor").

Field.Label: Include Station ID in Name:
Field.Description: Append last 6 chars of PASSKEY to device names — useful if running multiple gateways.

Field.Label: Stale Data Timeout (seconds):
Field.Description: Mark device offline if no data received for this many seconds. Minimum 60. Default: 300 (5 minutes).

Field.Label: Battery Low Threshold:
Field.Description: Battery value at or below this triggers low battery status. Most Ecowitt sensors use 0=OK / 1=Low. Default: 1.

Field.Label: Expected Update Interval (seconds):
Field.Description: Match your gateway upload interval. Used for documentation only — does not affect processing.

Field.Label: Expected PASSKEY (optional):
Field.Description: If set, only pushes containing this exact PASSKEY are accepted. Loaded from IndigoSecrets.ECOWITT_PASSKEY first; this field is the fallback if that file is absent. Use the value shown on the Main Gateway device's `passkey` state. Leave blank to accept any.

Field.Label: Enable Pushover Alerts:
Field.Description: Send a Pushover notification when any sensor battery goes low. Requires the Pushover plugin to be installed and enabled. Can also be toggled from the Plugin menu.

Field.Label: Pushover Device Name:
Field.Description: Optional: name of a specific Pushover device to receive alerts. Leave blank to send to all your devices.

Field.Label: Enable LDS Water Level:
Field.Description: Enable support for the LDS01/LDS04 laser distance sensor used to measure water tank levels. Can also be toggled from the Plugin menu.

Field.Label: Tank Height (mm):
Field.Description: Total internal height of your water tank in millimetres. The sensor measures distance from the sensor down to the water surface; this value converts it to a water level percentage. Default: 1000mm (1m).

Field.Label: Enable Timestamp in Logs:
Field.Description: Prefix all log messages with [HH:MM:SS]. Disable if you prefer Indigo's own column timestamps only. Also toggleable from the Plugin menu.

Field.Label: Enable Debug Logging:
Field.Description: Log detailed HTTP request and data processing information. Useful for troubleshooting new sensors.

Field.Label: Log Raw POST Data:
Field.Description: Log the complete raw POST payload from the gateway. Use when diagnosing new or unknown fields.

Field.Label: Log Device State Updates:
Field.Description: Log every device state update. Can be verbose at short update intervals.

Field.Label: 1. Open WS View Plus app on your phone
2. Menu - Device List - [Your Gateway] - More - Weather Services - Customized
3. Set: Server IP = [Indigo server IP above]
4. Set: Port = [HTTP Server Port above] Path = /data/report/ Protocol = Ecowitt
5. Set Upload Interval to your preferred value (30-60 seconds recommended)
6. Save and wait - Indigo devices are created automatically on first data receipt
